objects.label: Laptop
objects.note: Where most of the research code gets written. Usually late, lo-fi in the background, the desk lamp turned all the way down.

objects.label: Notebook
objects.note: A new one every quarter. Most of the good ideas live here before they live in any repo.

objects.label: Coffee
objects.note: Filter, black, no sugar. Almost always cold by the time I remember it.

objects.label: Candle
objects.note: Lit when a paper is stuck. Useful as a focus timer, useful as an excuse to slow down.

objects.label: Wedding ring
objects.note: Married to the love of my life. The best co-author I have.

objects.label: Window · canal
objects.note: The Brouwersgracht. On clear nights you can see the gables across the water. Most of my running routes start by following it east.

objects.label: Books
objects.note: Sartre on the desk. Boom Latinoamericano on the shelf, especially Borges. A short stack of paper drafts on top of everything.

objects.label: Half marathon medal
objects.note: 1:43:53 in Amsterdam, October 2024. Mostly there to remind me that the long, boring middle of anything is where the good ideas show up.

objects.label: Running shoes
objects.note: On their second resole. Most weeks they cover the loop from Vondelpark to the Amstel and back.

objects.label: 瓜子 · the cat (planned)
objects.note: Future cat, name already chosen: 瓜子 (sunflower seeds), because I cannot stop snacking on them. The chair is already reserved. So is the corner of the desk.

objects.label: Tennis rackets · Pure Drive + Blade
objects.note: Two rackets. The Pure Drive is the easy one (mine, mostly). The Blade kills the wrist but unlocks the most when you find the sweet spot. That one belongs to my wife. Saturday mornings on the public courts.

objects.label: Wok · Sichuan stir-fry
objects.note: I'm from Sichuan, so the spice is in the blood. No pot, no slow simmer: just the wok, high heat, garlic, chili, doubanjiang, the things that announce themselves on the first breath.

objects.label: Tulips
objects.note: Different colors, restocked every spring at the Bloemenmarkt. My wife and I once filled the apartment with them. The Dutch spring without tulips wouldn't be the Dutch spring.
